steps.prompt: Which word is the main verb?

hints.senatus: 'senatus' is a noun meaning 'the senate' — the subject of the sentence
hints.uti: 'uti' is a conjunction here meaning 'that' — introduces the subordinate clause
hints.videret: 'videret' is a verb but in the subordinate clause, not the main verb

hints.Present indicative: Present would be 'decernit' — 'Decrevit' is the perfect form

hints.cresco, crescere, crevi, cretum: These are the parts of 'crescere' (to grow)
hints.cerno, cernere, crevi, cretum: These are the parts of 'cernere' (to discern) — the base verb, but 'decernere' has its own compound forms
hints.credo, credere, credidi, creditum: These are the parts of 'credere' (to believe)

hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'videat' — 'videret' with -ret ending is imperfect subjunctive
hints.Imperfect indicative: Imperfect indicative would be 'videbat' — 'videret' is subjunctive
hints.Perfect subjunctive: Perfect subjunctive would be 'viderit' — 'videret' is imperfect subjunctive

steps.prompt: Why is the subjunctive used with 'videret'?
steps.target: Substantive clause of commanding (indirect command after 'decrevit')
choices: Substantive clause of commanding (indirect command after 'decrevit')

hints.Purpose clause: Purpose clauses also use subjunctive, but this is introduced by 'uti' after a verb of decreeing — it's an indirect command
hints.Result clause: Result clauses use 'ut' + subjunctive but follow expressions of degree — 'decrevit' triggers an indirect command
hints.Indirect question: Indirect questions use interrogative words (quid, cur, etc.) — 'uti' here introduces a command

hints.Genitive: Genitive would be 'consulis' — 'consul' is nominative, subject of 'videret'
hints.Vocative: Vocative would look the same, but in this reported decree 'consul' is the subject

hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-er — 'consul, consulis' is 3rd declension
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns have gen. -us — 'consul, consulis' has gen. -is (3rd)
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es — 'consul, consulis' is 3rd declension

steps.prompt: What famous decree is Cicero referencing?
steps.target: The senatus consultum ultimum — emergency decree
choices: The senatus consultum ultimum — emergency decree

hints.A routine administrative decree: The formula 'ut consul videret' (that the consul should see to it) is the language of the emergency decree (SCU)
hints.A declaration of war: Declarations of war followed different procedures — this is the SCU, granting extraordinary powers to the consul
hints.A decree of exile: Exile required a specific legal process — the SCU authorized the consul to take any measures to protect the state

translation: The senate once decreed that the consul should see to it.
